title: Impacts of tropical forecast errors on weeks 3–4 extreme precipitation predictions over California during winter 2022–23
relevance_summary: Directly addresses subseasonal (weeks 3–4) forecast skill for extreme precipitation in California by examining tropical error impacts, bridging S2S prediction and extreme event forecasting with large-scale atmospheric conditioning.

title: Improving Subseasonal to Seasonal Global 2-m Temperature and Precipitation Forecasts Using a Deep Learning–Based Postprocessing Approach
relevance_summary: Develops deep learning postprocessing for S2S probabilistic forecasts of temperature and precipitation at weeks 1–6 using ECMWF reforecasts; directly addresses ML/XAI for S2S prediction skill improvement and identifies key predictors by lead time.

title: Divergent Mechanisms of High Health-Risk Compound Hot and Dry Events across China’s Early and Late Warm Seasons
relevance_summary: Investigates seasonal and regional drivers of high health-risk compound hot–dry events across China using large-scale circulation modes and teleconnections; directly addresses compound event drivers and S2S predictability mechanisms with event-based framing.

title: Toward Reducing Uncertainty in Simulating Temporal Clustering of Extreme Precipitation in Morocco: Insights from High-Resolution GCMs
relevance_summary: Develops process-based framework to detect and evaluate temporal clustering of extreme precipitation in Morocco; directly addresses compound consecutive precipitation extremes and their representation in high-resolution GCMs at subseasonal timescales.

title: Improving subseasonal‐to‐seasonal prediction of the ENSO–East Asia teleconnection: A dynamical–statistical model approach
relevance_summary: Improves subseasonal-to-seasonal prediction of ENSO-East Asia teleconnection using dynamical-statistical hybrid model; directly addresses S2S predictability and teleconnection-based forecast skill.

title: Seasonal hindcasts to assess the hazard of meteorological drought over Europe: A multimodel approach
relevance_summary: Multimodel seasonal hindcasts assessing meteorological drought hazard over Europe with verification focus, directly addresses S2S drought prediction skill and ensemble methods for hazard assessment.

title: Prediction Skill of ENSO and ENSO Teleconnections in NorCPM: A Comparative Analysis with C3S
relevance_summary: Evaluates seasonal prediction skill of ENSO and associated teleconnections using two operational forecast systems (NorCPM and C3S), directly relevant to S2S predictability and verification methods.

title: Impact of the Ensemble-Derived Background Error Covariance in the Hybrid 4DEnVar Data Assimilation Framework on the Boreal Summer Predictability of a Global Numerical Model
relevance_summary: Examines sensitivity of hybrid 4DEnVar data assimilation to ensemble-derived background error covariance and its impact on boreal summer forecast skill, relevant to S2S prediction methodology and predictability.

title: The Role of Equatorial Waves in Triggering Precipitation Extremes in the Maritime Continent
relevance_summary: Reviews convectively coupled equatorial waves and their role in driving precipitation extremes at subseasonal timescales in the Maritime Continent; addresses predictability sources and forecasting challenges for S2S timescales.

title: Statistical Properties of Stratiform and Convective Rain Rates under Seasonal Changes and MJO Propagation at Jakarta, the Maritime Continent
relevance_summary: Analyzes stratiform vs. convective rainfall characteristics and their variation with the MJO at subseasonal timescales in Jakarta; relevant to understanding S2S predictability sources and precipitation type discrimination for compound event prediction.

title: Pacific Extratropical Precursors and Dynamic Mechanism of Two Types of Summer ENSO: Continuing and Emerging ENSO
relevance_summary: Identifies precursors of ENSO diversity (continuing vs. emerging) and dynamical mechanisms involving extratropical forcing and air–sea interaction, directly relevant to S2S predictability sources and teleconnection pathways.

title: Drivers of Marine Heat Waves in the North Pacific Ocean
relevance_summary: Analyzes drivers of marine heat waves in the North Pacific linked to ENSO and PDO with atmospheric and oceanic feedback mechanisms; relevant to compound extreme event drivers and S2S predictability sources.

relevance_summary: Examines interannual variability of marine heat waves in the South Indian Ocean associated with ENSO using mixed-layer heat budget analysis; relevant to compound extremes and S2S predictability from teleconnections.

title: Extreme Marine Heat Waves in the Tropical Atlantic during Boreal Early Spring 2024: Major Role of Shortwave Radiation and Mixed Layer Depth
relevance_summary: Investigates extreme marine heat waves in tropical Atlantic during early spring 2024 with multi-regional drivers and links to El Niño and South Atlantic dipole; relevant to compound extremes and S2S predictability mechanisms.

title: Impacts of TC and MJO on El Niño Evolution: Revisiting the Formation Mechanism of 1986/87–1987/88 Two-Year El Niño
relevance_summary: Reveals role of high-frequency tropical cyclone and MJO forcing in sustaining multiyear ENSO events; directly relevant to S2S predictability sources and understanding of compound seasonal climate phenomena.

relevance_summary: Identifies dominant intraseasonal modes of winter surface air temperature in the extratropical Northern Hemisphere linked to Rossby wave dynamics; relevant to S2S predictability sources and teleconnection mechanisms.

title: The Impact of Arctic Amplification on Midlatitude Winter Weather: Assessing the Stratospheric Pathway in a Warming Climate
relevance_summary: Examines Arctic amplification and its stratospheric pathway influence on midlatitude winter weather and subseasonal extreme events in warming climate; relevant to S2S predictability sources and teleconnections.

title: Projected Future Changes of Ningaloo Niño under Global Warming

title: Regional Heavy Snowfall Events over the Northwestern Tibetan Plateau Dominated by Distinct Circulation Patterns

relevance_summary: Analyzes MJO moisture mode and tropical wave activity in counterintuitive MJO events; relevant to understanding S2S predictability sources and teleconnection mechanisms.

relevance_summary: Investigates interaction between MJO and easterly waves as precursors to extreme precipitation events at subseasonal timescales over Central America; relevant to S2S predictability sources and compound event drivers.

relevance_summary: Examines Southern Ocean–tropical eastern Pacific teleconnection strength modulating multidecadal variability and forced response; relevant to understanding S2S/seasonal predictability sources and remote climate interactions.

relevance_summary: Explores combined effects of ENSO and Arctic sea ice on North American climate, demonstrating teleconnection modulation relevant to understanding sources of seasonal predictability and their interaction with large-scale atmospheric states.
